key_insight: PathReportEval introduces a standardized benchmark and a new clinical metric, CRQS, to measure whether pathology report generation models are factually correct—not just lexically similar.
clinical_relevance: For pathology report generation in precision oncology, this framework can better detect missed diagnoses, hallucinations, and wrong tumor attributes than BLEU, ROUGE, or METEOR.
method: Benchmarks 4 representative report-generation methods on 3 datasets: TCGA, HistAI, and REG 2025.
Compares 3 pathology foundation encoders: CONCHv1.5, UNI2-h, and H-Optimus-1.
Uses CRQS to score clinical fact coverage, key information recall, hallucination rate, and clinical discordance.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that conventional NLP metrics are weakly aligned with clinical correctness, while CRQS exposes meaningful differences across models and encoders on standardized evaluation.
watch_item: CRQS depends on mapping free-text reports into structured clinical attributes, so performance may vary with extraction quality and how well it generalizes across institutions and reporting styles.
paper_title: PathReportEval: A Systematic Benchmark for Pathology Report Generation
